Dark cream viscosity test check update





White chocolate viscosity test check update

Candon Watt,

Do you think this visco difference will cause problems with one shot?
Gondola is throwing chocolates out on the exit for TG4, we are cleaning and will restart back up.
Started back up.
Both buffer tanks are currently set minimum 20% and maximum 40% later we will adjust to minimum 10% max 30% and then 2nd shift will monitor, I also asked PTL guys to keep them at 50%.
Did we already did that flap on depositor 2? Let's do that while the line is down please

White chocolate viscosity test check update
Dark cream viscosity test check update

Good morning Itzel Chavez, 
 
As we discussed can you communicate where we are with the buffer tank levels and starting to manage melting chocolate?
 
Let's also have it as an action in the shift exchange Rich Brierley, so Marcos MontanoTyson Fausett can continue to manage it as we run this towards the end of 2nd shift into 3rd.
So far we have not had any issues with Silver on any of the rolls for FW.
The number of bubbles is acceptable, but the dark cream is exposed and staining the film. Itzel Is there anything we can do to improve this?
Preston Elmer did Chiara responded to you?

You have 2 options for this kind of issues, if you checked first that you don’t have that many air bubbles into the depositor hopper, and that your temper is good. If you are good there, you can or increase your deposition speed for the filling or decrease the deposition start delay for your filling as well.
Itzel Chavez

Tempering chart check update 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Organoleptic test check update 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications. But We still have bubbles at the bottom of the bars.

Weight test check update 


The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

Flow wrap ckeck update 


The flow-wrap packaging is in good condition; it looks good and is very well sealed.

Countdown. Tyson Fausett
Bars are looking good on Depositor 1
Bars are coming out have bad half good we have paused the line
These are the alarms that are most likely associated with the issue.

They are also coming out of the Gondola this way. We are positioning people at the Flow wrap entrances To take off bad bars. Once the Gondola is empty we will start the WDS slow and take the bad bars off there.

Tyson Fausett this was a good catch.

Preston Tregaskis how do we know that we correctly bounded this issue? As in, caught the earliest of the problem and the latest of the problem.
The pallet that we were using has been set off to the side to be put in hold. We are starting a fresh pallet with good product

Noticed my PTL operators were both using white Spatulas for boxes. I coached them and found them the correct tool.
Angel Cabrera let's get on this tonight. I know we just talked, but posting here for visibility.
Thank you for catching that Tyson. No white utensils are for product, not packaging. Do you have someone cleaning that spatula already?
Tyson Fausett please have the team monitor the silver lining on TG5

Tyson Fausett

What’s your 20?
on my way to you
2nd jam of the day

Mid shift

We are at -0.5 temper on depositor 4 Chad

Report on Line ML4 Production Run: Operations have generally proceeded satisfactorily, meeting established quality standards. Product weights remain within required parameters; ingredients have been verified against specifications; tempering falls within acceptable ranges; and screening revealed no issues requiring special mention. Furthermore, the packaging and palletizing areas are in full compliance with established procedures and requirements.
However, regarding organoleptic inspections, the following observation was noted and requires reporting: We continue to observe small black chocolate specks on the base of the bars—present in varying quantities—which, in a few instances, are scattered across the base. Upon breaking the piece, these specks do not detach or crumble away (as illustrated in the accompanying photos). While this is currently deemed acceptable, we will continue to monitor this aspect closely through ongoing collaboration between the Production and Quality departments
Made some small adjustments that Erandy suggested. Will update when they come out in 30
We have had 5 jams already. I have asked maintenance to take a look up top to see if there is an issue
Atp magnet✅️

Thank you Tyson Fausett

How does the product look before demolder?  Can you send a picture?
Still seeing jams Tyson Fausett?
This pallet of 4 black barrels for the chocolate push passed visual and ATP
Tote of the FEA 60g molds passed ATP and visual

A piece fell off inside of the scraper. I am not sure what it does exactly but it sits underneath the molds. I am having my team clean it and leave it off for now since it is t necessary to run so maintenance can inspect it for damage.
It fell off on the left side. The other picture is of the one sitting on the right side still on place
Are all the pieces of that area accounted for?
Yes. Nothing appears to be missing. Also this sits below the molds and we aren’t using the scraper so there shouldn’t be any impact to the product.
That’s good news. Thank you for the clarification

We have had one more since. Maintenance did adjust one of the rails. It was in to far turning the boxes sideways sometimes. The operator has seen multiple times on the video where it is sending two in side by side.
Bars are looking better.

0-3% un buffer 2, caused no backs on bars.  We’ve raised it to 1-4%
We’ve been down since then with further faults
Please go up to 10-20%

If it is causing problems then it is not worth being that low.  

Thank you for testing the system.
We’re doing a reheat to try to clear faults and get product pumping through, it has air as well
It definitely was running ok at 5 percent we were done on Depositor 1. I lowered it  and stepped away. I should have stayed at buffer 2 and monitored the chocolate flow

Thank you for the details  

When you say you were done on Dep 1 do you mean depositing?

Or do you mean making adjustments.
You may want to go to 10-20% to get more fresh chocolate in buffer tank with the reheat.
We were done depositing on depositor 1, just needed to run out rest of line onto dep 4. We’re doing reheat and increasing percentage a bit
Replacement belt blue 🔵 small Machine 4
We are running

Awesome!
 
It sounds like you are going to be ahead and make the order versus the speed we have been at which is great news.
 
What speed did yall run WDS at last night?
We stopped the line bad temper we are doing another tests
Sorry speed we ran at was 11-13 depending on what was going on. If the TG were running good we ran at 13
Can yall send a picture of the temper graph where you are documenting slope?
Frederick before completely shutting down. Let's check with IMS that we have all of our numbers.
That's what I am working on right now trying to get accurate numbers
I was told wds hold 42,000 bars so we are trying to get final numbers
The graph doesn’t show any of your bad tempers and if you had them it should. 🤷
We need 13 pallets we have 9 pallets per the math we need 30k bars and I was told WDS holds 42k so we have stopped depositing at depositor 1 around 4:10 am
We’re back up running
WDS runs fine at 5% on buffer any lower and you have to keep a close eye on it. If depositor 4 runs out of chocolate causes many alarms

Thank you Frederick Peairs. 

Let us know a status on FG and what is left in the machine when you can. 

Will be good to see if all the calculations worked out. 

If we need to deposit more to finish the order we will want to have that ability.
We are going to be short approximately 100 cases we are starting back up now 3rd is completing a capture test for 1st shift now since we have to add new chocolate and cookies to the Inclusion hopper

Itzel Chavez 
Please get with IMS for numbers before starting the clean.
 
Thank you

Thank you for catching that Itzel!

I took everything we had wrote on the board from the last flush put it in a document. The document also lives where all the SU/SD checklist are its named Flush if you need another copy.
 
Ray i am not sure if you want to review the document and add more to it if needed?

Good morning team

For item 30448 Preston is waiting for response for the version issue because we only have v5 instead of v6 that the spec calls. As of now, we are good to used v5 because that’s what we have right now but Preston would update the plan once has more information Rose Smuin, Chad Stone

Thanks!
Kevin Del Valle I haven’t seen anything regarding a version issue. Can you message me what the issue is? Is this for a pouch or flow wrap?

I'll add you to the chat. I reached out to Anisa already, based on that meeting we had with them and your team a while back, I understood the version changes should go from then to your team to ensure the specs and BOM get updated accordingly.
Ya since we don’t have V6 pouches coming in until July we will use the V5.
Kevin Del VallePreston Tregaskis the spec for 30448 is now updated with the V5 pouch. Please refresh your teams and get the spec and show operations.
Jenny Medina - can you please help with this since Kevin is on 3rd shift right now? Just make sure everyone on our team and production is aware of the updated spec.
Sure

Verification for the lot/BB date and checkweigher completed.
Pouch is seal well. Correct lot&BB date. No product is found from the previous run. Case label is correct. 32 pieces are inside the pouch. No major issues to report

Top suctions on the pouch opening isn’t reading that it’s open all the way. Which is preventing it to deposit I informed maintenance and let them know from the side it look like the bottom portion of the suction cup isn’t touching the pouch completely which could be causing it to not get a tight suction.

Everything was fine with the molds, only the pins are dirty.

The molds alarm be active again.This is the fourth time the same alarm has been triggered.

This is the fourth time the alarm has been triggered. The molds are very dirty. We will replace them with the clean molds we have available and continue washing molds to replace additional ones as they become available.
The fifth time the alarm has been activated

Grey Boscan, Rich Brierley

Do we have individuals standing at shakers and wiping down the part of the mold that attaches to the pins?
Please still be detailed at checking when the alarm comes on as we don’t want to have a mold jam to create a larger issue.
We will continue verifying the molds each time the alarm is triggered, together with Maintenance and QA,

There is chocolate buildup underneath the back side of the hopper that is contacting the molds. We will try to clean the buildup to see if the issue improves. Molds that are heavily soiled will be removed, washed, and gradually replaced with clean molds.
The area was cleaned as thoroughly as possible; however, it is difficult to access with the hopper installed. We will continue monitoring.
Weight of chocolate in the target
Organoleptic testing: appearance, taste, texture and odor are good

We are going to start testing the flow wrap rolls that were reported as not working. I asked the operator to run them and let me know whether they work or not so we can verify if all of them have glue issues.

Maintenance is currently inspecting the damage to the temperature sensor.
The mold alarm is reactivated.
Are we actively dripping on the back side getting the molds dirty?
The lot code is correct
The weight of the supersack is on target.

We checked with Maintenance and do not see anything dripping from the back side. What we are seeing now is liquid chocolate coming out from the front side. We are going to empty the hoppers first and then investigate what is causing it.
Maintenance fix the temper machine
We removed the plate to inspect it because the chocolate is leaking but the gasket looks fine.

Shift start up checklist
We got a bad temperature. We have stopped putting chocolate in the molds while we do another temperature check.
If this one comes back bad we will have to do a reheat. Unless Robert Villarrealdo you think we can lower the crystallization
We got .4 temper we are starting back up

Yes you can lower the crystallization - adjust it .2 at a time like the mixing water.
 
It takes much longer to see results from those adjustments though. Where mixing water you can retest in less than 10 minutes, the crystallization takes about 15 to 20 minutes
Temper is good, we have started putting chocolate in the molds.

Still a good idea to lower crystallization. 
 
Nathan/Imperial, 
I should have checked while I was there, can you verify the temperature of the buffer tank?
Buffer tank chocolate temperature
Robert Villarreal did you see their picture? And it’s okay to start lowering crystallization by .2 every 30mins you think?

Getting toasty I see - might be good to watch and ensure it doesnt' get too hot Nathan,
But yes go ahead and begin adjusting crystallization. I think with all of the issues every shift has been having and being at the limit of mixing water adjustments it would be good to try and find a better spot for the crystallization. 
I foresee us having to increase mixing water temps as we decrease crystallization but lets see what happens. 
Do not allow crystallization to go below 80.0  without calling it out please/ There is a limit to how low we should go but lets hard stop at 80.0 for now
Understood I’ll make sure to pass it on to the other shifts as well

Nathan Gevenois Preston Tregaskis will you please investigate the excessive stuff rework that’s been occurring on this line today and for this run? Will you please also create a standard of what can be reworked and what cannot so that we can hold the teams to it.
These are too scuffed to get reworked.
Angel CabreraAngel is working on updating standards and best practices. Thank you!

We realized that the handle controlling the flow of chocolate was sending all chocolate to the trough. We adjusted the handle so that most chocolate goes to the trough, but some goes to the bottomer, and the chocolate usage has gone down. We are now averaging 6.0 grams per piece with good bottom coverage still. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

A sensory evaluation was conducted on One Shot Caramel MMK Item 21115 produced during the 2nd shift on Friday, May 29. This production was affected by the electrical interruption that occurred during the shift.
During the evaluation, an excessive vanilla flavor was detected, resulting in a deviation from the expected organoleptic profile of the product. This variation contributed to a slightly bitter flavor note and altered the standard caramel taste characteristics.
The collected samples will be submitted to the R&D team for further evaluation and analysis. R&D will assess the impact of this deviation and determine the appropriate conclusions and recommendations. Curt Willis Charity Young Miriah Manzanares Italu Martinez Marcos Jimenez Dakota Adair

Report on Line ML4 Production Run: Operations have generally proceeded satisfactorily, meeting established quality standards. Product weights remain within required parameters; ingredients have been verified against specifications; tempering falls within acceptable ranges; and screening revealed no issues requiring special mention. Furthermore, the packaging and palletizing areas are in full compliance with established procedures and requirements.
However, regarding organoleptic inspections, the following observation was noted and requires reporting: We continue to observe small black chocolate specks on the base of the bars—present in varying quantities—which, in a few instances, are scattered across the base. Upon breaking the piece, these specks do not detach or crumble away (as illustrated in the accompanying photos). While this is currently deemed acceptable, we will continue to monitor this aspect closely through ongoing collaboration between the Production and Quality departments
